The fact that there is no checking account reduces the estimated default probability, because it reduces the estimated default probability, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [1]
The fact that critical credit history or other existing credits functions as a primary determinant for default probability, because it functions as a primary determinant for default probability, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that the requested credit amount is directly associated with an increased likelihood of default, because it is directly associated with an increased likelihood of default, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.

final_explanation_ko:
본 AI가 예측한 결과에 따르면,\n계좌 잔액 부족은 채무 불이행 가능성을 낮추는 신호로 인식되지만, 본 모델은 이를 위험 증가 요인으로 판단합니다 [1].
신용 문제 이력이나 기존 대출이 채무 불이행 확률에 주요 영향을 미친다는 것은 위험 증가 신호로 알려져 있지만, 본 모델은 이를 위험 감소 요인으로 해석합니다 [2].
대출 금액이 채무 불이행 확률 증가와 직결된다는 점은 위험 증가 신호로 이해되지만, 본 모델은 이를 위험 감소 요인으로 평가합니다 [3].
전반적으로 이 고객은 채무 불이행 위험이 낮은 수준으로 평가됩니다.
